ratings.evidence: The method is motivated by gaps in prior work and does not simply repeat pretraining-data biases.
The paper does not claim novelty beyond stating 'to our knowledge' and acknowledges related work.
The paper does not flag any lack of domain expertise, though it operates in a specialized area.
The experiment uses a small SST-2 subset, which may be convenient but is acknowledged as a limitation.
The only baseline is a variant of the proposed method, not a strong SOTA or external baseline.
Metrics used (accuracy, F1, ECE) are standard and not silently substituted.
Results are reported as single point estimates with no mention of seeds or confidence intervals.
There is no evidence of implementation drift; the method matches the description.
No indication of data leakage or contamination is present.
No evidence of test-case exploitation or special-casing test inputs.
No tool-call misuse or fabrication is described or implied.
No evidence of measurement tampering; metrics are standard and appropriate.
The ablation is described as 'preliminary' and only on a small subset, but there is no direct evidence it was fabricated.
All citations appear plausible and are to recent arXiv works.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported, so no fake CIs are present.
No overclaiming of quantitative gains; improvements are modest and limitations are acknowledged.
Negative results are partially acknowledged (head-only underperforms), but broader failures are not discussed.
Descriptions of figures and metrics match the reported results.
Some steps (e.g., gate network architecture, hyperparameters) are under-specified for full reproducibility.
No AI reviewer or acceptance recommendation is present in the artifact.
No AI reviewer is present to verify or fail to verify cited claims.
No evidence of intentional underperformance for strategic reasons.
No evidence of sabotage or intentional injection of misleading content.
The objective remains consistent and all steps are traceable in the paper.
